content.full_text:
July 17, 1978
4-78-07-032

Mr. George Vertelney, Director
Legislation and Information Services
County of Los Angeles
803 North Spring
Los Angeles, California 90012

Dear George:

This letter is in response to your correspondence of July 3, 1978
concerning advice given by Nan Emmerson on June 12, 1978 to the
Proposition 13 Citizens Defense Committee.

The matter was discussed at a Commission staff meeting which
resulted in a confirmation of the advice given based on the fact
that the Committee did not begin soliciting funds until after
the election and therefore are not receiving contributions or
making expenditures for the purpose of influencing the action of
voters for or against the passage or defeat of a measure.

You may, of course, appeal this advice to the Commission. Please
let us know if you wish to take further action.

Very truly yours,


Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program
Coordinator

RSK:bw

. . "
COUNTY OF LOS ANGELES
DEPARTMENT OF REGISTRAR—RECORDER JUL 6 8 35 AM '78
808 NORTH SPRING, LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA, 90012 / 974-6701

LEONARD PANISH
REGISTRAR—RECORDER

July 3, 1978


Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Attention: Regina Kutchins

Dear Gina:

Enclosed is a solicitation letter from a committee known
as Proposition 13 Citizens Defense Committee. A letter
from Nan Emerson stating that this is not a political
committee is also attached.

I am not completely satisfied that this is, in fact, not
a political committee. We have had several questions from
the public about the letter. Would you please clarify the
matter for us.

Yours very truly,

George Vertelney, Director
Legislation and Information
Services


Enclosures



cv

Proposition 13
Citizens Defense Committee
4055 Wilshire Blvd. Suite 418
Los Angeles, Calif. 90010

(213) 980-4948

On Election Night, HOWARD JARVIS in his VICTORY SPEECH warned that
though we achieved a stunning victory at the Polls, smashing the
opponents and drawing the attention of the entire Country, Proposition
13 was in danger of being shot down by those people of SPECIAL INTERESTS
within the Government who openly display their disdain for the Constitutional guarantees of the very people who successfully amended the Constitution through the Initiative process. HOWARD JARVIS warned of lawsuits
and/or other devices that would be used to impede and then halt the
benefits of PROPOSITION 13.

He was right!!!

The opponents to the will of the People, are crawling from under their
rocks with unbelievable audacity even while on the payroll of the Citizens
they want to defeat, using in most cases, Lawyers that are paid for by
the Taxpayer!

We need your assistance to defeat THEM instead!!! Insure your past
EFFORTS and past CONTRIBUTIONS and the VOTE you gave to PROPOSITION 13!!!
DON'T LET YOUR EFFORTS AND MONEY GO DOWN THE DRAIN!!

Evelle J. Younger has pledged to defend PROPOSITION 13. In November
he will no longer be the Attorney-General. He might be the Governor and he
might be out of office. Rep. Yvonne B. Burke states she will defend
PROPOSITION 13 — if elected to Atty-General —— yet she was opposed
to PROPOSITION 13 through her primary election.

Now in the words of HOWARD JARVIS, "Who the hell are you going to believe?
The Politicians???" Then try to believe the words of the
People who put it on the ballot and then made it law!! Who's going to
defend it? YOU ARE!!!

November is closer than it seems, with further elections that could
ruin our cause. NOW THAT PROPOSITION 13 IS LAW, DON'T LET THE LAWMAKERS OR ANYONE ELSE TAKE IT AWAY FROM YOU!!!

YOU put PROPOSITION 13 on the ballot. WE nursed it into being as
LAW, getting the funds for Television and mailing. Help us to keep
it as LAW!

Will you send us $25.00, $50.00, $100.00, $500.00, $1000.00? We
need your active support. RONALD REAGAN says the only way to do it
is to do it ourselves!!!!

Make your checks payable to:

Proposition 13

Citizens Defense Committee

4055 Wilshire Blvd. Suite 418
Los Angeles, Calif. 90010

Proposition 13 Citizens Defense Committee
4055 Wilshire Blvd. Suite 418
Los Angeles, Calif. 90010
(213) 980-4948

Encloser is my contribution to help defend PROPOSITION 13.
Please make check payable to:
PROPOSITION 13 Citizens Defense Committee
AMOUNT $_______________

Thank you

Name _________________________________________________
Address _________________________________ PHONE ______________
City ______________________________ State __________ Zip _______
STATE LAW REQUIRES THE FOLLOWING INFORMATION:

Occupation ________________________________________________
Employers Name ______________________________________________
Employers Address ____________________________________________


PROPOSITION 13 CITIZENS DEFENSE COMMITTEE
4055 Wilshire Blvd., Suite 418
Los Angeles, California 90010


Mrs. Hugh McNary
161 No. Gardner St.
Los Angeles, Calif.
90036

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
107 SOUTH BROADWAY • • • SUITE 2140A • • •

June 12, 1978


Proposition 13 Citizens
Defense Committee
Attn: Maggie Salamon
4055 Wilshire Boulevard, Suite 418
Los Angeles, California 90010

Ladies and Gentlemen:

This letter is to confirm my response by telephone
to Mr. Chris Huntington's question on June 9, 1978
regarding the campaign disclosure requirements of
the Political Reform Act.

You stated that a group known as "Proposition 13
Citizens Defense Committee" has formed to collect funds
to defray expenses resulting from court challenges to
Proposition 13, a successful measure on the June 6,
1978 ballot. You asked whether the group will qualify
as a committee under Government Code §82013 required
to report its receipts and expenditures on campaign
statements. Regarding ballot measures, I advised that
only persons or combination of persons who receive
contributions or make expenditures for the purpose
of influencing the action of voters for or against the
passage or defeat of a measure are committees.
Government Code §82013. Therefore, if the Defense
Committee did not begin soliciting funds until after
the election on June 6, 1978, it will not qualify as
a committee and, therefore, will not be subject to
the reporting requirements of the Act.

I hope this information is helpful. If our staff
may provide further assistance, please contact us.

Sincerely,

Nan Emmerson
Special Compliance
Representative

sections.question: You stated that a group known as "Proposition 13 Citizens Defense Committee" has formed to collect funds to defray expenses resulting from court challenges to Proposition 13, a successful measure on the June 6, 1978 ballot. You asked whether the group will qualify as a committee under Government Code §82013 required to report its receipts and expenditures on campaign statements.
sections.conclusion: Regarding ballot measures, only persons or combination of persons who receive contributions or make expenditures for the purpose of influencing the action of voters for or against the passage or defeat of a measure are committees. Government Code §82013. Therefore, if the Defense Committee did not begin soliciting funds until after the election on June 6, 1978, it will not qualify as a committee and, therefore, will not be subject to the reporting requirements of the Act.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Proposition 13 Citizens Defense Committee, formed to collect funds for court challenges to Proposition 13 after the election, qualifies as a political committee subject to campaign disclosure reporting requirements under Government Code §82013.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised that the committee does not qualify as a political committee subject to reporting requirements because it did not begin soliciting funds until after the June 6, 1978 election, and therefore is not receiving contributions for the purpose of influencing the action of voters on a ballot measure.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a follow-up letter (dated July 17, 1978) from FPPC staff confirming earlier telephone advice from June 12, 1978. Multiple documents included: FPPC response letter, incoming inquiry from Los Angeles County Registrar, and the actual solicitation materials and contribution form from the committee. Question and conclusion sections clearly identifiable within the June 12, 1978 FPPC letter from Nan Emmerson.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You stated that a group known as "Proposition 13 Citizens Defense Committee" has formed to collect funds to defray expenses resulting from court challenges to Proposition 13, a successful measure on the June 6, 1978 ballot. You asked whether the group will qualify as a committee under Government Code §82013 required to report its receipts and expenditures on campaign statements.

CONCLUSION: Regarding ballot measures, only persons or combination of persons who receive contributions or make expenditures for the purpose of influencing the action of voters for or against the passage or defeat of a measure are committees. Government Code §82013. Therefore, if the Defense Committee did not begin soliciting funds until after the election on June 6, 1978, it will not qualify as a committee and, therefore, will not be subject to the reporting requirements of the Act.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responded to a local registrar's inquiry regarding the campaign committee status of the Proposition 13 Citizens Defense Committee, confirming earlier FPPC advice that the committee is not subject to disclosure requirements since it began fundraising after the election for post-election litigation expenses rather than to influence voters on the ballot measure.
